Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Hussain
Message:
feat(MIL-149): direct WorkOS Magic Auth on login.cjipro.com + sign-out cleanup

Replaces the AuthKit-domain hop in the sign-in/sign-out flow. Corp
proxies that block *.authkit.app on URL-category filters can no
longer break sign-in for partner users. Bug surfaced live during
demo with Hussain Barclays account on corp wifi.

WHAT SHIPPED
============

1. /sign-in/ flow (mil/auth/magic_link/src/sign_in.ts +
   sign_in_pages.ts)
   - GET  /sign-in/        — email entry form, our domain only
   - POST /sign-in/email   — POST api.workos.com/user_management/
                              magic_auth/send (Bearer auth)
   - POST /sign-in/code    — POST api.workos.com/user_management/
                              authenticate, grant_type=
                              urn:workos:oauth:grant-type:magic-auth:code
   - HMAC-signed state across the email->code hop, 10-min TTL.
     Separate kind:"signin" payload from MIL-63 OAuth state to
     defend against state-type confusion.
   - Cookie + sessions row + partner_profiles row write reuses the
     existing /callback side-effect chain — MIL-65/66/151/152
     invariants hold.
   - DIRECT_SIGNIN flag (default "true" post-cutover) makes GET /
     302 to /sign-in/ instead of the AuthKit redirect.

2. Sign-out simplification (mil/auth/magic_link/src/index.ts)
   - POST /logout success now 302s directly to https://cjipro.com/
     with Set-Cookie (clear) + Clear-Site-Data: "cookies","storage".
   - AuthKit /api/logout front-channel hop removed entirely.
     Rationale: DIRECT_SIGNIN routes all sign-ins through Magic Auth
     API server-to-server, so AuthKit never sets a session cookie
     in the user agent — there's nothing for /api/logout to clear.
     WorkOS's /api/logout silently drops return_to and falls back
     to a dashboard-configured Homepage URL with poor discoverability,
     producing error.workos.com landing pages. Owning the post-logout
     hop ourselves removes the dashboard dependency.
   - Server-side WorkOS session revoke + D1 sessions row delete
     still fire — MIL-161 lifecycle preserved.
   - GET /logout no-cookie + POST /logout no-cookie also 302 cjipro.com
     (idempotent, consistent UX).

3. Single-hop bouncer redirect (panel finding)
   - edge-bouncer + app-cjipro LOGIN_URL flipped from
     https://login.cjipro.com/ to https://login.cjipro.com/sign-in/.
     Collapses bouncer->/-> /sign-in/ double-302 into one hop.
     Saves a round-trip and removes one phishing-pattern signature
     on bank corp proxies that flag redirect chains.

4. Partner sign-in href patch (14 cjipro.com pages)
   - All Partner sign-in links now carry
     ?return_to=https%3A%2F%2Fapp.cjipro.com%2Fportal so users
     always land on /portal post-auth instead of inheriting whatever
     bouncer-stamped path they tried to reach first.

5. Audit event types (mil/auth/audit/src/types.ts)
   - 7 new typed events: magic_link.signin.{code_sent,
     invalid_email,send_failed,verify_failed,success,
     invalid_code,state_expired}.

PANEL FINDINGS DELIBERATELY NOT ACTIONED
=========================================

Cookie prefix rename (panellist 3): __Secure-cjipro-session WITH
Domain=.cjipro.com was flagged as RFC 6265bis violation. Verified
against the spec directly: only __Host- (Section 4.1.3.2) forbids
Domain. __Secure- (Section 4.1.3.1) explicitly permits Domain.
Our cookie is fully compliant. Renaming would touch 25 files,
force re-sign-in, deliver zero compliance value. Skipped.

id_token_hint approach (panellist 2): would let WorkOS /api/logout
honor return_to without dashboard fallback. Doesn't apply to our
flow — Magic Auth grant_type=magic-auth:code does not return an
id_token regardless of scope. Bypassing /api/logout entirely (this
commit) is the cleaner answer for our architecture.

WorkOS Homepage URL dashboard config: was load-bearing pre-this-commit
(determined post-/api/logout landing). No longer in our path. Can
be left set as defence-in-depth or unset for cleanliness.

DEPLOY ORDER
=============

magic-link Worker shipped during demo (version d41c0be0, current).
edge-bouncer + app-cjipro need redeploy to pick up the LOGIN_URL
change:
  cd mil/auth/edge_bouncer && npx wrangler deploy
  cd mil/auth/app_cjipro && npx wrangler deploy

TESTS
======
- magic-link: 223/223 pass (verified pre-deploy)
- edge-bouncer: typecheck clean post-fixture-update
- app-cjipro: typecheck clean post-fixture-update

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Hussain
Message:
feat(MIL-67a): WorkOS webhook ingestion for passkey + auth events

Phase A of MIL-67. WorkOS handles the WebAuthn ceremony itself once
admin enables it in the dashboard; this commit adds our side: a
signature-verified webhook receiver that records every WorkOS event
to audit_events for observability + future filtering.

Why generic now, typed later: WorkOS event-type names aren't in
codebase context, and guessing them risks dropping passkey events
silently. Recording everything as workos.webhook with the real event
type in `reason` lets us narrow to specific event types (Phase B)
once we observe what actually fires.

- magic_link/src/webhooks.ts: HMAC-SHA256 signature verification per
  WorkOS spec (WorkOS-Signature header t=<ts>,v1=<hmac>; HMAC of
  `<ts>.<raw_body>`; reject when timestamp skew > 5min); constant-time
  compare; parses event into { id, event, data, created_at }
- magic_link/test/webhooks.test.ts: 11 tests (valid sig accepted,
  bad sig + tampered body + wrong secret + replay + missing secret +
  malformed header + non-JSON + missing fields all rejected with the
  right reason and HTTP status)
- magic_link/src/index.ts: new POST /webhooks/workos route. Rejection
  → console.log (don't pollute audit chain with unauth requests);
  acceptance → audit row with reason=event.type, detail=event.id
- magic_link/src/env.ts: WORKOS_WEBHOOK_SECRET (optional; absent
  endpoint 503s, fail closed)
- audit/src/types.ts: workos.webhook event type
- mil/auth/MIL67_PASSKEYS.md: 4-step admin runbook + Phase B plan +
  failure modes + security properties

Deploy state:
- magic-link e7a73fe4 — POST /webhooks/workos live, returns 503 until
  WORKOS_WEBHOOK_SECRET is set via `wrangler secret put`

Smoke test: unauth POST returns 503 (correct fail-closed behavior).

To activate: admin enables WebAuthn in WorkOS dashboard → adds
webhook → wrangler secret put WORKOS_WEBHOOK_SECRET. Detailed steps
in MIL67_PASSKEYS.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Hussain
Message:
feat(MIL-63): magic-link Worker — chunks 1+2+3

Pure-logic core (state/authorize/exchange/cookie/callback) plus a
fetch-handler entrypoint with routes for /, /callback, /logout,
/healthz, /favicon.ico. Validates an HMAC-signed OAuth state
param, exchanges the AuthKit code via WorkOS User Management,
sets __Secure-cjipro-session on .cjipro.com, 302s back to the
return_to preserved in state. Open-redirect guard on return_to.

Chunk 3: route bindings commented in wrangler.toml for both
Workers (edge_bouncer briefings + magic_link login custom
domain) with full cutover procedure inline — activation is a
manual deploy-day step, not an auto-flip.

Not yet deployed. User provisions WORKOS_CLIENT_SECRET and
STATE_SIGNING_KEY via `wrangler secret put`, then
`wrangler deploy`. Route cutover demotes the MIL-59 placeholder
Worker in the same operation.

44 tests on magic_link (state/authorize/cookie/callback/index).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Hussain
Message:
feat(MIL-49): email subject to Voice of Customer framing + Dear Team greeting

- Subject: "Sonar PDB · 22 Apr · Barclays — App Crashing · CLARK-2 · Panel
  recommended" -> "Voice of the Customer: Barclays App Experience (Open
  Sources)". Drops operator-speak and internal codes; "Open Sources"
  signals OSINT scope to a Barclays risk/compliance reader. Subject is
  still immutable per Teams inbox rules — just a new locked value.
- Greeting: per-recipient "Dear {display_name}" -> hardcoded "Dear Team,"
  in both plaintext and HTML builders. Lets the same email fan out to a
  cohort without per-recipient rendering drift. display_name still exists
  in distribution.yaml, just no longer rendered.

CLAUDE.md also sweeps in pending doc drift from 2026-04-22 work: Ask CJI
Pro v1 (MIL-39..MIL-48) BACKLOG->BUILT, first MIL-49 section, Apr 22
session arc.

Test-fired 2026-04-23 11:52 GMT+1 to hussain.x.ahmed@barclays.com;
email_log.jsonl row 11 carries the new subject cleanly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Description: Discovered a potential basic authorization token provided in a curl command, which could compromise the curl accessed resource.
Author: Hussain
Message:
chore: PULSE migration sync — manifest, CLAUDE.md, Phase 4 layer

- manifests/system_manifest.yaml: jira_project KAN→PULSE, last_updated 2026-03-18
- CLAUDE.md: all KAN- ticket refs replaced with PULSE-, stale root manifest removed
- .gitignore: extended to exclude root-level *.csv and *.parquet
- Add Phase 4 components: Dockerfile, Dockerfile.streamlit, docker-compose.yml
- Add app/ pages (daily briefing, scorecard, investigation queue, Ask CJI, voice panel)
- Add ci/ governance and HDFS compliance tests
- Add twin_refinery/ dbt project (staging model, profiles, HDFS→PG loader)
- Add conductor/ Airflow 3.x DAG scaffold
- Add hadoop-env/ HDFS config
- Add src/agents/llm_client.py and model_router.py
- Add .gitlab-ci.yml CI/CD pipeline

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
